steps.title: Identify the proxy's core requirement
steps.content: For D. polymorpha to work as a proxy for O. mykiss in bioaccumulation testing, their accumulation patterns for CeO2-NPs must be comparable. A proxy relies on reliably mirroring the behavior of what it replaces.

steps.title: Find what breaks the proxy relationship
steps.content: Choice B directly undermines this by showing that CeO2-NP uptake rates differ between the two species in ways researchers don't yet understand. If accumulation rates are different, test results from the mussel won't reliably predict results in the trout—this defeats the entire proxy hypothesis. The other choices either support comparability (A shows both species accumulate with consistent individual variation), address a different issue (C describes nanoparticle types accumulated, not CeO2-NP rate similarity), or identify an advantage of mussels unrelated to proxy validity (D's fewer negative effects). That matches choice B — B is correct.
